title: Overview
text: package meant forecast salmon returns using sibling regression. core functions produce model average predictions (.e., ensembles) dynamic linear models (DLMs) sibling predictors. package also functionality forecast using DLMs penalized-complexity priors, can alternative model averaging.

title: Overview
text: can install development () version sibregresr GitHub :
code: install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("wdfw-fp/sibregresr") library(sibregresr) #> Error in get(paste0(generic, ".", class), envir = get_method_env()) :  #>   object 'type_sum.accel' not found

title: Overview
text: One must provide data frame age-specific returns either brood- return year, using specific column names. example: multiple Stocks included data, forecasting conducted stocks. Returns youngest age class produced, rather used predictor forecasting second youngest age class. forecast youngest age class (e.g. “Age3”), data need augmented “dummy” column yet younger age class (e.g., “Age2”).
code: summer_chinook_2024 |> head(5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>   BroodYear  Age3  Age4  Age5  Age6 Stock          #>       <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>          #> 1      1984    NA    NA    NA  1549 Summer_Chinook #> 2      1985    NA    NA  9378   730 Summer_Chinook #> 3      1986  3197  7869  9145   287 Summer_Chinook #> 4      1987  2699  4450  3816   335 Summer_Chinook #> 5      1988  2569  5310  6180   356 Summer_Chinook brood_to_return(summer_chinook_2024)|> head(5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #> # Groups:   Stock [1] #>   Stock          ReturnYear  Age3  Age4  Age5  Age6 #>   <chr>               <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Summer_Chinook       1989  3197    NA    NA    NA #> 2 Summer_Chinook       1990  2699  7869  9378  1549 #> 3 Summer_Chinook       1991  2569  4450  9145   730 #> 4 Summer_Chinook       1992  3862  5310  3816   287 #> 5 Summer_Chinook       1993  1405  7495  6180   335

title: Overview
text: Forecasts can developed using forecast_fun() function. arguments call set defaults, includes 7 models ensembles, log transforms response predictor, scale predictor response prior fitting. Models can fit normal space specifying transformation = identity inverse_transformation = identity. response predictors can Z-scored prior fitting specifying scale_y = TRUE scale_x = TRUE. perf_yrs argument specifies many one-year-ahead forecasts conduct order assess performance model. wt_yrs argument specifies many one-year-ahead forecasts use performance-based model averaging (e.g., weighting based MAPE). wt_yrs NULL, number perf_yrs also used wt_yrs. However, don’t encessarily need . One specify perf_yrs = 15 wt_yrs = 1 conduct 15-year performance evaluation approach weighting models based performance solely previous year. also possible use stretching window rather sliding window (.e., include available years performance /weighting) setting perf_yrs /wt_yrs total number years data. perf_yrs = 15 wt_yrs = 15 perf_yrs + wt_yres + 1 = 31 years one-step ahead forecasts component (.e., ensemble) model needed performance-based model weighting. first 15 years performance-based ensemble forecasts uses weights based performance components previous 15 years. call forecast_fun() produce warning number forecasts needed greater number data points minus five (assuming least five years available produce first forecast).
code: forecast<-forecast_fun(   df = summer_chinook_2024,   include = c("constIntOnly", "tvIntOnly", "tvSlope", "constLM",     "tvCRzeroInt", "constCRzeroInt", "tvInt"),   transformation = log,   inverse_transformation = exp,   scale_x = FALSE,   scale_y = FALSE,   perf_yrs = 15,   wt_yrs = NULL, ) #> [1] "Time for model fitting was 16.8 secs"

title: Overview
text: output call forecast_fun() function list two data frames. “fits” data frame information model fitting useful models failed fit. “forecasts” data frame row every year, age, stock, model, including model averages. Included data frame observed forecasted returns well several performance metrics. Definitions variables data frame prodived documentation performance_weights() function, can accessed call ?performance_weights(). total forecast returns summed across ages given model included sum component ages “Age” field. Fore example, forecasting returns ages 4, 5, 6, total value 15 “Age” field. example information available “forecasts” data frame – predictions different ensemble models recent year, ordered MAPE year, performance metrics.
code: forecast$forecasts |> dplyr::filter(ReturnYear==max(ReturnYear),grepl("weight",model_name)) |> dplyr::select(Stock,Pred,MAPE,RMSE,MPE,MEr) |> dplyr::arrange(Stock,Age,MAPE) |> dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(where(is.numeric),round)) #> # A tibble: 16 × 8 #> # Groups:   Stock, Age, model_name [16] #>      Age model_name    Stock           Pred  MAPE  RMSE   MPE   MEr #>    <dbl> <chr>         <chr>          <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #>  1     4 RMSE_weight   Summer_Chinook 22678    39 18993    13  1878 #>  2     4 MAPE_weight   Summer_Chinook 22606    40 19475    14  2290 #>  3     4 MeanSA_weight Summer_Chinook 22794    43 20716    18  3885 #>  4     4 AICc_weight   Summer_Chinook 23323    47 24932    26  6311 #>  5     5 MAPE_weight   Summer_Chinook 15140    39  9690    16  -686 #>  6     5 MeanSA_weight Summer_Chinook 14880    40  9491    20   411 #>  7     5 RMSE_weight   Summer_Chinook 14884    40  9615    19    87 #>  8     5 AICc_weight   Summer_Chinook 13264    44 10272    29  3109 #>  9     6 MAPE_weight   Summer_Chinook   936 14321  4429 14244 -2072 #> 10     6 AICc_weight   Summer_Chinook   921 14479  4538 14398 -2181 #> 11     6 RMSE_weight   Summer_Chinook   966 14820  4363 14746 -2007 #> 12     6 MeanSA_weight Summer_Chinook   968 15007  4345 14933 -2004 #> 13    15 MAPE_weight   Summer_Chinook 38682    28 26788     5  -468 #> 14    15 RMSE_weight   Summer_Chinook 38528    29 26685     6   -42 #> 15    15 MeanSA_weight Summer_Chinook 38643    30 27909     9  2292 #> 16    15 AICc_weight   Summer_Chinook 37509    38 33700    17  7239

title: Overview
text: Prediction intervals based standard deviation one-year ahead predictions (.e., forecasts) observations log space. number years included standard deviation calculations minimum number years available values specified perf_yrs. example, perf_yrs = 15 three years ensemble forecast generated prior given year, three years used calculate forecast standard deviation prediction intervals, however, 20 years ensemble forecast available, recent 15 years used. “n_sd” field output call forecast_fun() provides number years used calculate prediction intervals forecast. number years used calculate weights ensemble forecast conducted way (.e., available “wt_yrs”) “n_wts” field output gives number years used. relevant ensembles based AICc weighting based AICc value models fit current year.

title: Overview
text: ’ve also included plotting function predictions given model 50% 90% prediction intervals. haven’t spent much time refining though, area development.  Also function high-level table. Age Forecast 50%CI 90%CI MAPE RMSE MPE MEr model_name 4 22,606 16,986 - 30,086 11,259 - 45,391 40 19,475 14 2,290 MAPE_weight 5 15,140 11,419 - 20,073 7,610 - 30,119 39 9,690 16 -686 MAPE_weight 6 936 181 -  4,830 17 - 51,231 14,321 4,429 14,244 -2,072 MAPE_weight Total 38,682 30,728 - 48,695 22,065 - 67,814 28 26,788 5 -468 MAPE_weight ’ve started report template, quite bit work probably needed. forecasting already conducted call forecast_fun() , can generate report call like: can also feed forecast_report() function raw data arguments feed forecast_fun() order one function call. disadvantage get output one model (e.g., “MAPE_weight”) wheras fit save output call forecast_fun(), can investigate render report multiple models desired without refit.
code: make_plot(forecast$forecasts,"MAPE_weight") make_table(forecast$forecasts,"MAPE_weight") forecast_report(forecast$forecasts,                 mod_name = "MAPE_weight",                 output_file = "Forcast report.docx"                 )

title: Overview
text: Forecast_fun() wrapper around mod_funs(),setup_data(),fit_mods(), performance_weights() functions. mod_funs() generates list functions fit individual models. list passed , setup_data(), generates nested data frame row combination subset data (.e., number years cutoff end) model. row represents model must fit prediction made, function kicks process using list-column workflow. fit_mods() function data transformation steps conducts model fitting. Finally, performance_weights() function uses fitted model objects produce forecasts, assesses performance, generates ensembles, generates preduction intervals. similar output generated Forecast_fun() generated following code difference Forecast_fun() function appends observations years forecasts produced output.
code: mod_list<-mod_funs(c("constIntOnly", "tvIntOnly",                      "tvSlope", "constLM", "tvCRzeroInt", "constCRzeroInt", "tvInt"))  setup<-setup_data(summer_chinook_2024,mod_list,n_forecasts = 31)  fits<-fit_mods(setup)  forecasts<-performance_weights(fits,perf_yrs = 15)

title: Overview
text: Dynamic linear models penalized complexity can fit including “PenDlm”. highly recommended set scale_x = TRUE scale_y = TRUE fitting penalized models. wt_yrs = 1 reduce number subsets data fit models model averaging happening. penalized complexity model puts penalties mean coefficients βt\beta_t year tt standard deviation steps random walk. coefficients : $$\beta_t = \beta_{t-1} + \omega_t \\ \omega_t \sim \mathcal{N}(0,\sigma) $$ model puts exponential-gamma penalties β‾\bar{\beta} σ\sigma $$ \bar\beta, ~\sigma \sim \text{exp}(\lambda)\\ \lambda \sim \text{Gamma}(\text{Shape}=10,~ \text{Scale}=1) $$ Additionally, 0.05*log(β‾,σ)0.05*\text{log}(\bar\beta,\sigma) allβ‾\bar\betas σ\sigmas added log-likelihood keep values shrinking small cause numerical problems optimization.
code: pen_dlm_forecast<-forecast_fun(   df = summer_chinook_2024,   include = c("PenDlm"),   transformation = log,   inverse_transformation = exp,   scale_x = TRUE,   scale_y = TRUE,   perf_yrs = 15,   wt_yrs = 1, ) #> [1] "Time for model fitting was 7.5 secs"  pen_dlm_forecast$forecasts |> dplyr::filter(ReturnYear==max(ReturnYear),model_name=="PenDlm") |> dplyr::select(Stock,Pred,MAPE,RMSE,MPE,MEr) |> dplyr::arrange(Stock,Age,MAPE)|> dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(where(is.numeric),round)) #> # A tibble: 4 × 8 #> # Groups:   Stock, Age, model_name [4] #>     Age model_name Stock           Pred  MAPE  RMSE   MPE   MEr #>   <dbl> <chr>      <chr>          <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1     4 PenDlm     Summer_Chinook 24045    33 15593     7 -1667 #> 2     5 PenDlm     Summer_Chinook 14678    39  9468    19   186 #> 3     6 PenDlm     Summer_Chinook   872 14014  3998 13944 -1734 #> 4    15 PenDlm     Summer_Chinook 39594    23 22288     1 -3216

title: Overview
text: One can change predictors penalized dlm model providing data frame predictor values return year column called “ReturnYear” brood year “BroodYear” joining salmon return data. example formula model provided mod_funs() function covariate data fit_mods() function, can passed forecast_fun() function well. specifying formula, “x” used represent sibling predictor. formula specified include sibling predictor well PDO NPGO two years prior fish return. theory, penalized complexity priors shrink coefficients colinear predictors, ok include . shape scale parameters gamma prior can controlled penDLM_gamma_shape penDLM_gamma_scale arguments mod_funs() forecast_fun(). fraction log σ\sigmas added log-likelihood can controlled penDLM_regu argument.
code: covariates_24 |> head(5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 11 #>   ReturnYear lag1_PDO lag2_PDO lag1_NPGO lag2_NPGO lag1_fall_Nino3.4 #>        <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1       1961   0.563   NA          0.806    NA                -0.141 #> 2       1962  -0.109    0.547      1.09      0.795            -0.376 #> 3       1963  -0.808   -0.151     -0.482     1.09             -0.395 #> 4       1964   0.0579  -0.875     -0.495    -0.528             0.808 #> 5       1965  -0.496    0.0225     0.282    -0.541            -0.988 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: lag2_fall_Nino3.4 <dbl>, smolt_sock <dbl>, #> #   lag1_log_socksmolt <dbl>, lag2_log_socksmolt <dbl>, pink_ind <dbl> pen_dlm_forecast_cov<-forecast_fun(   df = summer_chinook_2024,   include = c("PenDlm"),   transformation = log,   inverse_transformation = exp,   scale_x = TRUE,   scale_y = TRUE,   perf_yrs = 15,   wt_yrs = 1,   covariates = covariates_24,   penDLM_formula =formula("y~ x + lag2_PDO + lag2_NPGO") ) #> [1] "Time for model fitting was 13.5 secs"  pen_dlm_forecast_cov$forecasts |> dplyr::filter(ReturnYear==max(ReturnYear),model_name=="PenDlm") |>   dplyr::mutate(model_name="PenDlm_cov") |>  dplyr::select(Stock,Pred,MAPE,RMSE,MPE,MEr) |> dplyr::arrange(Stock,Age,MAPE)|> dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(where(is.numeric),round)) #> # A tibble: 4 × 8 #> # Groups:   Stock, Age, model_name [4] #>     Age model_name Stock           Pred  MAPE  RMSE   MPE   MEr #>   <dbl> <chr>      <chr>          <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1     4 PenDlm_cov Summer_Chinook 24178    33 15564     7 -1731 #> 2     5 PenDlm_cov Summer_Chinook 14687    40 10187    17  -620 #> 3     6 PenDlm_cov Summer_Chinook   872 13574  4624 13499 -2145 #> 4    15 PenDlm_cov Summer_Chinook 39737    24 22739     0 -4497

what: More general description of the modeling process
title: Overview
text: first step forecasting returns given stock ya,ty_{,t} age aa year tt fit models observed returns year t−1t-1. models available package variations sibling regression model time-varying intercept slope: {log(ya,t)=αt+log(ya−1,t−1)βt+vt,vt∼𝒩(0,Vt),αt=αt−1+wα,t,wα,t∼𝒩(0,Wα,t),βt=βt−1+wβ,t,wβ,t∼𝒩(0,Wβ,t), \left\{ \begin{aligned} \mathrm{log}(y_{,t}) & = \alpha_t + \mathrm{log}(y_{-1,t-1}) \beta_t + v_t, & \quad & v_t \sim \mathcal{N}\left(0, V_t\right), \\ \alpha_t & = \alpha_{t-1} + w_{\alpha, t}, & \quad & w_{\alpha, t} \sim \mathcal{N}\left(0, W_{\alpha, t}\right), \\ \beta_t & = \beta_{t-1} + w_{\beta, t}, & \quad & w_{\beta, t} \sim \mathcal{N}\left(0, W_{\beta, t}\right), \end{aligned} \right. αt\alpha_t βt\beta_t intercept slope log-transformed returns previous age previous year, respectively, allowed vary across years random walks process error variances wα,tw_{\alpha, t} wβ,tw_{\beta, t} respectively. residual vtv_t assumed normally distributed around zero variance VtV_t. Together vague prior distribution values α0\alpha_0 β0\beta_0 (slope intercept prior first year) equations define “full” sibling regression models. However, “full” model may overly complex optimal prediction, difficult fit practice. models simplified versions full model : 1) slope assumed constant time (.e., wβ,t=0w_{\beta, t}=0); 2) intercept assumed constant time; 3) slope intercept assumed constant time; 4) intercept assumed zero (.e., αt=0\alpha_t=0); 5) intercept assumed zero slope assumed constant time; 6) slope assumed zero; 7) slope assumed zero intercept assumed constant time. models fit using dlm package (Petris 2010) within sibregresr package. penalized version full DLM fit using RTMB (Kristensen 2024). Ensemble forecasts generated taking weighted average predictions multiple model. Ensemble model weights calculated model mm set (ℳ\mathcal{M}) 8 models based AICc, wm=e−0.5(AICm−AICmin)∑∈ℳe−0.5(AICi−AICmin), w_m=\frac{e^{-0.5\left(\mathrm{AIC}_m-\mathrm{AIC}_{\min }\right)}}{\sum_{\\mathcal{M}} e^{-0.5\left(\mathrm{AIC}_i-\mathrm{AIC}_{\min }\right)}}, based observed performance Perf\mathrm{Perf} previous years measured reciprocal mean absolute percent error (MAPE), root mean square error (RMSE), mean symmetric accuracy (MeanSA). wm=Perfm−1∑∈ℳPerfi−1, w_m=\frac{\mathrm{Perf}_m^{-1}}{\sum_{\\mathcal{M}} \mathrm{Perf}_i^{-1}},

previous_headings: More general description of the modeling process
title: Overview
text: Giovanni Petris (2010). R Package Dynamic Linear Models. Journal Statistical Software, 36(12), 1-16. URL https://www.jstatsoft.org/v36/i12/. Kristensen K (2024). RTMB: ‘R’ Bindings ‘TMB’. R package version 1.6, URL https://github.com/kaskr/RTMB. Petris, Petrone, Campagnoli. Dynamic Linear Models R. Springer (2009).

title: Sibling Regression Salmon Forecasting
text: package forecasting salmon returns using sibling regression. core functions produce model average predictions (.e., ensembles) dynamic linear models (DLMs) sibling predictors. package also functionality forecast using DLMs penalized-complexity priors, can alternative model averaging. See Overview article details information package functionality.

title: Sibling Regression Salmon Forecasting
text: can install sibregresr GitHub :
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("wdfw-fp/sibregresr")

title: Sibling Regression Salmon Forecasting
text: example fitting ensemble 7 variations sibling regression DLM evaluating performance 15 years. beta version plotting table function called view ensemble forecasts weights based mean absolute percent errors. See Overview article details examples.
code: library(sibregresr)  forecast<-forecast_fun(   df = summer_chinook_2024,   include = c("constIntOnly", "tvIntOnly", "tvSlope", "constLM",     "tvCRzeroInt", "constCRzeroInt", "tvInt"),   transformation = log,   inverse_transformation = exp,   scale_x = FALSE,   scale_y = FALSE,   perf_yrs = 15,   wt_yrs = NULL, ) #> [1] "Time for model fitting was 18.2 secs"  make_table(forecast$forecasts,"MAPE_weight") make_plot(forecast$forecasts,"MAPE_weight")

title: Table of predictors — covariates_24
text: tibble 65 rows 11 columns ReturnYear Brood year lag1_PDO lag2_PDO lag1_NPGO lag2_NPGO  lag1_fall_Nino3.4 lag2_fall_Nino3.4 smolt_sock lag1_log_socksmolt lag2_log_socksmolt pink_ind pssoble predictors: climate indices, sockeye smolt indices Boneville Dam, even-odd year index meants represent pink salmon cycle

code: fit_mods(   dat,   transformation = log,   scale_x = FALSE,   scale_y = FALSE,   covariates = tibble(ReturnYear = numeric(0)) )

title: Prep data for each Stock/Age/n_years and fit the models — fit_mods
text: dat tible returned setup_data function transformation transformation conducted response predictors. default log scale_x boolean whether scale predictor data prior fitting scale_y boolean whether scale response data prior fitting covariates data frame covariates add data. must contain "ReturnYear" field, used perform join salmon return data.

title: Prep data for each Stock/Age/n_years and fit the models — fit_mods
text: tible

title: Prep data for each Stock/Age/n_years and fit the models — fit_mods
text: Can take several seconds conducting maximum likeihood optimization many models (n mdel * n stocks * n forecasts)

title: generate forecasts — forecast_fun
text: generate forecasts

title: generate forecasts — forecast_fun
code: forecast_fun(   df = summer_chinook_2024,   include = c("constIntOnly", "tvIntOnly", "tvSlope", "constLM", "tvCRzeroInt",     "constCRzeroInt", "tvInt"),   transformation = log,   inverse_transformation = exp,   scale_x = FALSE,   scale_y = FALSE,   perf_yrs = 15,   wt_yrs = NULL,   covariates = tibble(ReturnYear = numeric(0)),   ... )

title: generate forecasts — forecast_fun
text: df Input data. Either return table brood table. Column names: Stock, ReturnYear (BroodYear), age (e.g., Age3). Must format. include vector names models include. Default options transformation transformation conducted response predictors. default log inverse_transformation inverse transformation conducted response prior fitting (default exp) scale_x boolean whether scale predictor data prior fitting scale_y boolean whether scale response data prior fitting perf_yrs maximum number years predictions include performance metrics. Set infinity use stretching window. wt_yrs number years one-step-ahed predictions use calculate performance-based model weights (.e., MAPE RMSE). null (default) number years used calculate weights used evaluate performance ensemble individual models. covariates data frame covariates add data. must contain "ReturnYear" field, used perform join salmon return data. ... arguments mod_funs performance_weights

title: generate forecasts — forecast_fun
text: list two dataframes: fits output call fit_mods() potentially useful information models failed fit. forecasts output call performance_weights() ensemble forecasts, forecasts component models, performance measures

title: generate forecasts — forecast_fun
text: Model options : constLM –	Sibling regression constant slope intercept. tvInt –	Sibling regression  time-varying intercept. tvSlope –	Sibling regression time-varying slope. tvIntSlope –	Sibling regression time-varying slope intercept. model inlcuded default generally converges one simpler model. words, exceedingly rare data support complex model including ensemble leads double weighting one simpler models. tvCRzeroInt –	Time varying "cohort ratio" model. Time varying slope, Intercept=0. constCRzeroInt –	Constant "cohort ratio" model. Constant slope, Intercept=0. tvIntOnly –	Time-varying Intercept-model. Random walk return, sibling predictor. constIntOnly –	Constant Intercept-model. Long-term average, sibling predictor. PenDlm – dynamic linear model like "tvIntSlope" penalized-complexity priors year--year variation mean covariate values. model also can accommodate additional predictors (e.g., environmental covariates). model included defaults

what: Render a report of forecast results — forecast_report
title: Render a report of forecast results — forecast_report
text: two options: 1) provide output call forecast_fun()performance_weights() 2) provide raw data specify arguments needed call forecast_fun()

code: forecast_report(   forecasts = NULL,   mod_name = "MAPE_weight",   stocks = NULL,   forecast_year = NULL,   df = NULL,   include = c("constIntOnly", "tvIntOnly", "tvSlope", "constLM", "tvCRzeroInt",     "constCRzeroInt", "tvInt"),   transformation = log,   inverse_transformation = exp,   scale_x = FALSE,   scale_y = FALSE,   perf_yrs = 15,   wt_yrs = NULL,   output_file = "Forcast report.docx",   output_dir = getwd(),   ... )

title: Render a report of forecast results — forecast_report
text: forecasts output call forecast_fun() performance_weights mod_name forecast model / ensemble type  report forecasts stocks vector names stocks include report forecast_year year report forecast df Input data. Either return table brood table. Column names: Stock, ReturnYear (BroodYear), age (e.g., Age3). Must format. include vector names models include. Default options transformation transformation conducted response predictors. default log inverse_transformation inverse transformation conducted response prior fitting (default exp) scale_x boolean whether scale predictor data prior fitting scale_y boolean whether scale response data prior fitting perf_yrs maximum number years predictions include performance metrics. Set infinity use stretching window. wt_yrs number years one-step-ahed predictions use calculate performance-based model weights (.e., MAPE RMSE). null (default) number years used calculate weights used evaluate performance ensemble individual models. output_file name output file output_dir directory output file ... arguments mod_funs performance_weights

Time varying slope, Intercept=0. constCRzeroInt –	Constant "cohort ratio" model. Constant slope, Intercept=0. tvIntOnly –	Time-varying Intercept-model. Random walk return, sibling predictor. constIntOnly –	Constant Intercept-model. Long-term average, sibling predictor. PenDlm – dynamic linear model like "tvIntSlope" penalized-complexity priors year--year variation mean covariate values. model also can accommodate additional predictors (e.g., environmental covariates). model included defaults

title: penalized DLM in RTMB — pen_dlm
text: function fit penalized dynamic linear regression model. penalized complexity model puts penalties mean coefficients \(\beta_t\) year \(t\) standard deviation steps random walk. coefficients : $$\beta_t = \beta_{t-1} + \omega_t \\ \omega_t \sim \mathcal{N}(0,\sigma)$$ model puts exponential-gamma penalties \(\bar{\beta}\) \(\sigma\). $$\bar{\beta},~\sigma \sim \text{exp}(\lambda) \\ \lambda \sim \text{Gamma}(\text{Shape}=10,~\text{Scale}=1)$$ Additionally, \(0.05*\text{log}(\bar{\beta}, \sigma)\) \(\bar{\beta}\)s \(\sigma\)s added log-likelihood keep values shrinking small cause numerical problems optimization.

title: penalized DLM in RTMB — pen_dlm
code: pen_dlm(   dat,   form = stats::formula("y~x"),   regu = c(0.05, 0.05),   gamma_shape = 10,   gamma_scale = 1 )

title: penalized DLM in RTMB — pen_dlm
text: dat dataframe response predictors form formula specifying linear predictor regu avoid standard deviations shrinking small running numerical issues, log standard deviation multiplied values added log-likelihood (subtracted negative log-likelihood). Defaults .05 .05 penalties mean coefficients year--year variability. gamma_shape shape paramter gamma prior expential distributions rate paramters. gamma_scale scale paramter gamma prior expential distributions rate paramters.

title: penalized DLM in RTMB — pen_dlm
text: list two components: fitted TMB model object, NLL linear predictors report(), well outfut call TMBhelper::fit_tmb(), used optimize model.

what: Calculates weighted average forecasts, performance metrics, and prediction intervals based on past performance — performance_weights
title: Calculates weighted average forecasts, performance metrics, and prediction intervals based on past performance — performance_weights
text: Prediction intervals based standard deviation one-year ahead predictions (.e., forecasts) observations log space. number years included standard deviation calculations minimum number years available values specified perf_yrs. example, perf_yrs = 15 three years ensemble forecast generated prior given year, three years used calculate forecast standard deviation prediction intervals, however, 20 years ensemble forecast available, recent 15 years used. "n_sd" field output call forecast_fun() provides number years used calculate prediction intervals forecast. number years used calculate weights ensemble forecast conducted way (.e., available "wt_yrs") "n_wts" field output gives number years used. relevant ensembles based AICc weighting based AICc value models fit current year.

title: Calculates weighted average forecasts, performance metrics, and prediction intervals based on past performance — performance_weights
code: performance_weights(   fits,   perf_yrs = 15,   wt_yrs = NULL,   transformation = log,   inverse_transformation = exp,   scale_y = FALSE )

title: Calculates weighted average forecasts, performance metrics, and prediction intervals based on past performance — performance_weights
text: fits data frame returned fit_mods function perf_yrs maximum number years predictions include performance metrics. Set infinity use stretching window. wt_yrs number years one-step-ahed predictions use calculate performance-based model weights (.e., MAPE RMSE). null (default) number years used calculate weights used evaluate performance ensemble individual models. transformation trnasformation conducted response prior fitting(default log) inverse_transformation inverse transformation conducted response prior fitting (default exp) scale_y boolean whether response scaled prior fitting

title: Calculates weighted average forecasts, performance metrics, and prediction intervals based on past performance — performance_weights
text: data frame predictions component models weighted average models based AICc, MAPE, RMSE weights.

title: Calculates weighted average forecasts, performance metrics, and prediction intervals based on past performance — performance_weights
text: AICc weights calculated : $exp(-.5 deltaAICc) / sum(exp(-.5 deltaAICc))$ MAPE RMSE weights calculated , example: MAPE^-1 / sum(MAPE^-1) model_name name model used forecast. includes compenent ensembles ensembles. Additionally, "best" model lowest MAPE perforance-evaluation period given year. Thgis included able see performance picking best-performaning model, whether ensemble one components, multiple years. "Model_name2" field indicates model "best" year. n_wts = number years forecasts observations used generate MAPE RMSE weights ensemble forecasts. Individual year metrics: Er = forecast error AEr = absolute forecast error PE= percent errr, APE = absolute percent error SQE = square error logQ = log (forecast/observed)- AlogQ = absolute logQ Multi-year bias metrics MEr = mean error MPE = mean percent error MeanlogQ = mean logQ Multi-year precision metrics RMSE = root mean square error MAer = mean absolute error MAPE = mean absolute percent error MeanSA = mean absolute logQ log_sd = standard deviation observed log space last n_years forecasts (.e., (sqrt(sum(logQ^2)/n))) n_sd = number years used calculate sample standard deviation observed
